narrative.canopy: At 2,600 metres, the air doesn't behave the way it does anywhere else in peninsular India. Cold enough for frost in January — a cold the rest of South India has never experienced. Mist sits in the valleys most mornings and dissolves by ten. The light when it comes is thin, almost northern.
narrative.land: The Nilgiris aren't solid forest the way the Western Ghats lower down are. They're a mosaic — open grassland breaking into pockets of stunted, wind-bent shola forest, then back to open ground. Tea was planted into this mosaic, not instead of it.
narrative.hand: Tea is picked, not harvested — two leaves and a bud, by hand, repeated through the growing season rather than once a year. A picker works the same rows for years and can identify, before a leaf is processed, which part of which garden it came from.
